event: Admitted to the obstetric department
Pre-term labor
Received 10 mg of nifedipine 4 times in 1 h
Pulmonary edema
Hypoxemia
Transfer to the intensive care unit (ICU)
Blood pressure 101/61 mm Hg
ECG revealed a sinus tachycardia (142 bpm)
Oxygen saturation 98% under 100% high flow oxygenation
Physical examination revealed a loud first heart sound, diastolic murmur, bilateral wheezing, and jugular venous distension
Fall in systolic blood pressure
Worsening desaturation (78%) with metabolic acidosis and hyperlactatemia (34 mg/dL)
Intubated and ventilated
Chest X-ray confirmed pulmonary edema
Cardiac echography revealed unknown severe mitral stenosis
Diagnosis of cardiogenic shock precipitated by administration of nifedipine in a pregnant woman with unknown severe mitral stenosis
Loop diuretics (furosemide) and full-dose heparin administered
Ultrasound examination showed no fetal distress
Transoesophageal echocardiography showed a severe rheumatic mitral stenosis
Spontaneous vaginal delivery occurred
Endometritis with sepsis
Ceftriaxone and ampicillin administered
Percutaneous double balloon mitral valvuloplasty performed
Mitral mean gradient reduced from 10 to 3 mmHg
Mitral area increased from 0.5 to 1.65 cm2
Pulmonary pressure normalized
Extubated
Recovery was rapid
